title: On the Migration Patterns of Wraith-Moths
text:
Three nights tracking the swarm along the Greywater has confirmed what the old hunters claimed and the college dismissed: the moths do not scatter at the first frost, they migrate, in formation, toward the high passes.

What draws them there remains unclear. Old Marrow's notes mention a similar pattern among carrion-moths near burial grounds, which raises an uncomfortable question about what, exactly, is waiting in those passes.
furtherStudies: Cross-reference with Old Marrow's bestiary, vol. III, and the burial records for the high passes garrison.
